summary:
An example of a placebo is a sham surgical procedure because it involves simulating real surgery without any operative intervention, relying solely on patient expectations. Unlike therapies with proven biological mechanisms—including cognitive behavior therapy, physiotherapy, or herbal remedies—that provide objective health benefits via pharmacological or psychosocial processes, shams do not alter physiology but instead generate improvement through belief alone.

Final Answer:

\boxed{\text{C}}

Okay, I need to summarize only what Agent1 said about why sham surgery is considered a placebo.

Only two sentences maximum focusing strictly on core clinical facts.

Sham surgery serves as a clear example of a placebo because it replicates a surgical procedure without actually performing any operation, thereby producing beneficial results driven exclusively by patient expectation. It differs fundamentally from active treatments like CBT, physiotherapy, or herbal medicine—all of which possess identifiable biological or psychological mechanisms leading to change—not just perceived benefit through suggestion.

This summary captures exactly how Agent1 defines and distinguishes placebos clinically using established research-based distinctions.
